reasoning: Confirmed. The sink at libs/functions.php:597 echoes $options['captcha_challenge_text'] into public HTML with no escaping. The sanitize_settings() callback (setup.php:277-396) never calls sanitize_text_field, wp_kses, or any escaper on captcha_challenge_text — it passes through via array_merge. The esc_html() noted in the analysis JSON (phase2_sources_sinks, line 2393) is located in interface/tab_captcha.php:60 (the admin settings form input), not in the frontend output path. Reachability is correct: captcha_fields_print() echoes the output on every public page with the builtin captcha enabled, so every visitor receives the payload. Auth is admin-only (manage_options), making this admin-stored-XSS — severity medium is appropriate per WordPress security standards.
